dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: SOC Toolkit - Security Operations Center
dashboard.backendDown: Backend unreachable - start the server with `uvicorn api.app:app`

contact.title: Contact the developer
contact.subtitle: Found a bug or a service issue? Send a note and it lands straight in my inbox.
contact.subject: Subject
contact.messagePlaceholder: What happened, steps to reproduce, and what you expected…

news.title: Security News
news.subtitle: Latest from curated threat-intel feeds.
news.error: Could not load news - the backend may be offline or the feeds unreachable.

qr.title: QR Analyzer
qr.subtitle: Decode a QR code and see where it really leads. The image is decoded in your browser; only the resulting link is checked.
qr.uploadDescription: A screenshot or photo containing a QR code
qr.readError: Could not read the image. Try a clearer screenshot of the QR.
qr.notFound: No QR code found in that image. Try a sharper, less cropped picture.
qr.noFlags: No obvious red flags in the payload.

urlCheck.checkedAgainst: Checked against VirusTotal and URLScan (results depend on your API keys in Settings).

link.title: Link Analyzer
link.subtitle: Unshorten a link and follow its redirects to see where it really lands - before you click it.
link.error: Could not analyze that link. Check the URL and that the backend is running.
link.bulkSubtitle: Paste multiple URLs (one per line) for an instant client-side risk-flag check - nothing is sent to the server.

file.title: File Inspector
file.subtitle: Static check of a download or setup - type mismatches, polyglots, embedded payloads, macros and hash reputation. The file is never run.
file.uploadDescription: Any file up to 100 MB - analyzed statically, never executed
file.error: Inspection failed. Make sure the backend is running.

phishing.title: Phishing Analyzer
phishing.subtitle: Upload an .eml file for automated phishing analysis
phishing.uploadDescription: Drag and drop an .eml file or click to browse
phishing.error: Analysis failed. Make sure the backend is running.

inbox.title: Live inbox triage
inbox.subtitle: Pull recent messages from a mailbox over IMAPS and analyze each. Credentials are used only for this request and never stored - use an app password.

logs.title: Log Analyzer
logs.subtitle: Upload log files for automated threat detection
logs.uploadDescription: Supports auth.log, access.log, Windows Security events
logs.error: Analysis failed. Make sure the backend is running.

ioc.title: IOC Extractor
ioc.subtitle: Extract indicators of compromise from files and text
ioc.uploadDescription: Supports PDF threat reports, .eml emails, plain text, HTML, CSV
ioc.error: Extraction failed. Make sure the backend is running.

iocPivot.title: IOC Pivot
iocPivot.subtitle: Pivot any indicator across OSINT sources - certificates, passive DNS, WHOIS, ASN, Shodan
iocPivot.error: Pivot failed. Make sure the backend is running.

yara.title: YARA Scanner
yara.subtitle: Scan a file against the bundled rule set. Matches surface the rule's metadata - severity, MITRE technique, and reference links when the rule author provided them.
yara.uploadDescription: Any binary or document. Matching happens against all compiled rules under backend/rules/yara/
yara.error: Scan failed. Make sure the backend is running.

sigma.title: Sigma Detection
sigma.subtitle: Inspect the rule corpus or evaluate a batch of normalised log events against every loaded rule.
sigma.rulesError: Failed to load Sigma rules. Is the backend running?
sigma.eventsHint: Shape matches the dicts emitted by our log analyzers (event_type, source_ip, etc.).
sigma.invalidJson: Events must be valid JSON (an array of objects).
sigma.mustBeArray: Top-level JSON must be an array of event objects.
sigma.evalFailed: Evaluation failed. Check the backend logs.
sigma.noTrigger: No rule triggered. This does not mean the events are safe - coverage is limited to the bundled rule set.
sigma.compileFailed: Compile failed - the rule may use an unsupported feature.

misp.title: MISP Enrichment
misp.subtitle: Paste a threat report; the toolkit extracts IOCs and checks each one against the configured MISP instance so novel indicators surface over the ones already known to the community.
misp.placeholder: Paste report, incident summary, or free-form text here…
misp.hint: IOCs are extracted locally; only confirmed IOC values are sent to MISP.
misp.pasteFirst: Paste a report or free-form text first.
misp.enrichError: Enrichment failed. Is the backend running?
misp.notConfiguredBody: Set MISP_URL and MISP_API_KEY in your .env to see enrichment results. Extraction still works below.
misp.noIocs: No IOCs found in the provided text.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Bring your own API keys to enable the enrichment integrations.
settings.info: Keys are stored only in your browser and sent with your own requests - never saved on the server. Self-hosted installs can set them in the server's .env instead.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Manage your account and avatar.
profile.avatarHint: PNG, JPG, WebP or GIF · up to 2 MB.
profile.licensePrompt: Have a license key? Redeem it to activate your plan.

auth.verifiedBody: Your account is active. You can continue to the app.
auth.verifyFailedBody: The link is invalid or already used. Sign in and request a new one.
auth.passwordUpdatedBody: You can sign in with your new password.
auth.setNewPasswordBody: Choose a new password for your account.
